name: Canard a L'orange
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to a very hot 450°F (232°C) to get skin nice and crispy.
2. Rub the inside and outside of the duckling with salt and pepper.
3. Place diced pulp and peel (only the orange part of the rind) from one orange and a sprig of thyme into the breast cavity. Carefully close the cavity with wooden toothpicks.
4. Place the prepared duck on a rack in a roasting pan, breast down. Fill the pan with hot water until it reaches 1 cup.
5. Add juice of 2 oranges plus their peel to the pan. After 30 minutes of baking, turn the duck over and pour juice of 2 more oranges over it.
6. Lower the oven temperature to 325°F (165°C) and baste the duck with drippings every 5 minutes. Bake for 1¼ to 1½ hours.
7. Orange Sauce: Strain the sauce from the dripping pan, removing all grease. Add one glass of port or sherry and bring to a short boil.
8. Garnish: Melt butter in a pan. Add sugar and stir until caramelized. Dip thinly sliced oranges (the remaining 2) into this mixture.
9. Place the duck on a hot platter and garnish with caramelized orange slices. This is the traditional French way of preparing duck.
